_fixture_doc: WS-7 Doc-06 SIDING gate fixture (PHASE 1). A realistic NY-market siding claim used by tests/test_doc06_siding.py. NY is chosen so house_wrap PRICES NATIVELY ($0.64/SF, Xactimate SDG WRAP) — the WS-7 CODE_SUPPLEMENT_FALLBACK_PRICED flag correctly stays SILENT here (it only fires in non-NY/PA/NJ markets that lack a native house_wrap rate; queue the Alfonso SDG WRAP export for those — follow-up, do NOT build here). Citations are the codebase-canonical sections verified against backend/code_compliance.py + backend/compliance_svg.py CODE_TO_ZONE + building_codes/state_codes.json (R703.1 corner rule, R703.2 WRB/house wrap, R703.3 siding install, R703.4 window/door flashing, R703.8 wall flashing) — no new citation is introduced. Unit prices are the NY native rates from pricing/nybi26.json (read OFF the frozen line item per B.7 — Doc 06 never re-resolves a price). compliance.user_role is 'contractor' (USARM default, UPPA-safe: factual code-requirement language, no advocacy/demand verbs). The siding test flips a deepcopy to user_role='public_adjuster' to exercise the PA/attorney advocacy path. carrier_line_items deliberately include siding on ONLY 1-2 elevations (Front + Right present; Left + Rear + all WRB/fanfold/corner/wall-flashing missing) so the OMITTED carrier-cross-reference marking has real omissions to render. This config is NOT a golden-corpus forensic snapshot — Doc 06 is not in WS-0, so adding it cannot perturb the 23/23.

carrier_line_items.note: Carrier approved Front elevation siding only.
Carrier approved Right elevation siding only — Left and Rear denied.
Carrier omitted house wrap entirely — R703.2 WRB is code-mandated behind all cladding.
Carrier omitted outside corner posts — corner trim must be removed to wrap WRB around the outside corner.
Carrier omitted wall flashing (R703.8) at openings and intersections — the #1 siding item carriers omit.

forensic_findings.executive_summary: Wind-driven hail and debris impact on June 14, 2025 fractured and cracked vinyl siding across all four elevations of this two-story residence. Impact marks, cracked panels, and dislodged courses are documented on the Front, Right, Left, and Rear walls. The damage is consistent with the verified storm and is the covered peril; it is not pre-existing wear.
forensic_findings.damage_summary: Vinyl siding shows storm-consistent impact damage on all four elevations: cracked and shattered panels, white-stress impact halos, and wind-dislodged courses. Damage is uniform with the storm track and inconsistent with age-related degradation.

code_violations.requirement: A water-resistive barrier (house wrap) shall be applied behind all exterior wall cladding, lapped shingle-fashion (min 2 in. horizontal lap, 6 in. joint lap), and shall be continuous — wrapping around outside corners.
code_violations.status: Carrier scope omits house wrap entirely; a single-elevation siding replacement cannot maintain a continuous WRB at the outside corners.

code_violations.requirement: Exterior walls shall provide a continuous weather-resistant exterior wall envelope; the WRB cannot terminate at an outside-corner joint.
code_violations.status: Wrapping the WRB around the outside corner requires removing the corner trim, which disturbs the connecting elevation's siding.

code_violations.requirement: Flashing shall be installed at exterior wall openings (windows, doors) and penetrations to prevent water entry behind the cladding.
code_violations.status: Carrier scope omits wall/opening flashing disturbed during siding replacement.

damage_thresholds.threshold: Repairable units exceed isolated-repair tolerance; new vinyl cannot color-match weathered 1996-era panels

differentiation_table.characteristics: Random impact halos, fractured panels, fresh break edges, directional with storm track
differentiation_table.observed: Present on all four elevations, concentrated on storm-facing Front and Right

differentiation_table.characteristics: Uniform chalking, fading, brittleness without impact halos
differentiation_table.observed: Not the dominant pattern; fresh fracture edges rule out pure age
differentiation_table.conclusion: Not the cause of the documented damage

key_arguments: Storm-caused vinyl siding damage is documented on all four elevations (the covered peril).
R703.2 requires a continuous water-resistive barrier that wraps outside corners; R703.1 prohibits terminating it at a corner joint.
New vinyl cannot color-match the weathered 1996 siding, so a 1-2 elevation patch leaves a visible mismatch.
conclusion_paragraphs: The documented storm damage, the R703.2/R703.1 continuous-WRB corner requirement, and the inability of new vinyl to match weathered panels together support full-elevation siding replacement as the code-compliant repair.

code_citation.title: Exterior Wall Covering — Vinyl Siding
code_citation.requirement: Siding shall be installed per the manufacturer's installation instructions and R703.1 (flashing at intersections); attachment per Table R703.3.2. A code-compliant installation includes the water-resistive barrier (R703.2) and wall flashing (R703.8).
code_citation.supplement_argument: The vinyl siding is storm-damaged on all four elevations (the covered peril). A code-compliant replacement under R703.3 requires the continuous water-resistive barrier (R703.2) wrapped around the outside corners per R703.1; this cannot be achieved on an isolated elevation without disturbing the connecting elevation's siding.
code_citation.jurisdiction: Residential Code of New York State (RCNYS, base code IRC 2018)

line_items.description: House wrap (weather-resistive barrier) - SDG WRAP, wraps outside corners

code_citation.title: Weather-Resistive Barrier (House Wrap)
code_citation.requirement: A water-resistive barrier shall be installed behind ALL exterior wall cladding, lapped shingle-fashion (min 2 in. horizontal lap, 6 in. joint lap), and shall be CONTINUOUS — it must wrap around outside corners and cannot terminate at a corner joint (manufacturer spec: wrap min 6 in. around corners).
code_citation.supplement_argument: House wrap is mandatory per R703.2 behind all cladding. Because the WRB must be continuous and wrap the outside corners per R703.1, it cannot be installed on the approved elevation without wrapping the corner — which requires removing the corner trim and disturbs the connecting elevation's siding. Full-elevation siding is therefore code-required, not additional scope.
code_citation.jurisdiction: Residential Code of New York State (RCNYS, base code IRC 2018)

manufacturer_specs.requirement: WRB must be continuous and wrap a minimum of 6 in. around outside corners; it may not terminate at a corner joint.

line_items.description: Fanfold insulation board - 1/4 in. (under vinyl siding)

code_citation.title: Siding Underlayment / Fanfold Board
code_citation.requirement: Vinyl siding shall be installed over a code-compliant substrate per the manufacturer's installation instructions; fanfold insulation board is the standard backer over which vinyl is fastened.
code_citation.supplement_argument: Fanfold board is the standard substrate for vinyl siding and is disturbed when the siding is removed; it must be replaced as part of a code-compliant R703.3 reinstallation.
code_citation.jurisdiction: Residential Code of New York State (RCNYS, base code IRC 2018)

line_items.description: Outside corner post - vinyl (corner trim, removed to wrap WRB around corner)

code_citation.title: Continuous Weather-Resistant Wall Envelope (Corner Rule)
code_citation.requirement: Exterior walls shall provide a continuous weather-resistant exterior wall envelope. The water-resistive barrier cannot terminate at an outside-corner joint; it must wrap continuously around the outside corner (R703.1 with R703.2).
code_citation.supplement_argument: The outside corner posts must be removed to wrap the WRB around the outside corner per R703.1. Removing the corner trim necessarily disturbs the connecting elevation's siding, so approving only one elevation leaves the WRB discontinuous at the corner — not code-compliant.
code_citation.jurisdiction: Residential Code of New York State (RCNYS, base code IRC 2018)

code_citation.title: Wall Flashing at Openings & Intersections
code_citation.requirement: Flashing shall be installed at exterior wall openings (windows, doors) and penetrations, and at wall-to-roof / wall-to-wall intersections, to prevent water entry behind the cladding (R703.4).
code_citation.supplement_argument: Wall and opening flashing is disturbed whenever siding is removed and must be replaced to maintain the water-resistant seal between the openings and the WRB. This is the #1 siding item carriers omit; without it, water infiltration behind the new cladding is inevitable.
code_citation.jurisdiction: Residential Code of New York State (RCNYS, base code IRC 2018)
